---
title: "Save a derived map with provenance"
subtitle: "Write a tSNR map whose Receipt rides the file."
jupyter: python3
sidebar: cookbook
---

## Goal

Compute a masked temporal-SNR map and write it so the Receipt is
embedded in the `.nii.gz` itself — no sidecar JSON, no manifest.

## Code

```{python}
from pathlib import Path
import sys
import tempfile

sys.path.insert(0, str(Path.cwd().parents[1] / "src"))

import nibabel as nib
import neuroim as ni

repo = Path.cwd().parents[1] if Path.cwd().name == "cookbook" else Path.cwd()
bold = ni.read_image(str(repo / "golden_tests/fixtures/tiny_bold.nii.gz"))
mask = ni.read_image(str(repo / "golden_tests/fixtures/tiny_mask.nii.gz"), type="vol")

tsnr = bold.temporal_snr(mask=mask)

out = Path(tempfile.mkdtemp()) / "tsnr.nii.gz"
nib.save(tsnr.to_nibabel(), str(out))        # Receipt embeds as NIfTI ecode-6 'comment'

out.stat().st_size > 0, tsnr.provenance.method_name
```

::: {.callout-note}
`to_nibabel().save(...)` is the documented write path that embeds the
Receipt. `ni.write_vol(tsnr, path)` saves the array but not the
Receipt, so use the `to_nibabel()` path when the file itself should
explain which operation, input image, and mask produced the map.
:::

## What's guaranteed

- The mask/BOLD frame is validated inside `temporal_snr` before the
  reduction runs.
- `to_nibabel()` writes exactly one `neuroim/receipt/v1:` extension,
  replacing any prior one; foreign `comment` extensions are untouched.

## What survives the file

The eight Receipt fields, byte-identical, in a NIfTI `comment`
extension (`ecode 6`), recoverable by [ten lines of `nibabel`
alone](../spec/receipt-nifti-extension.html):

```{python}
import json

img = nib.load(str(out))
ext = next(e for e in img.header.extensions if int(e.get_code()) == 6)
text = bytes(ext.get_content()).rstrip(b"\x00").decode("utf-8")
receipt = json.loads(text[len("neuroim/receipt/v1:"):])
sorted(receipt)
```

## Trapdoors

```{python}
#| error: true
from neuroim.neuro_space import NeuroSpace

wrong = NeuroSpace(tuple(mask.space.dim), spacing=(9, 9, 9))
bold.temporal_snr(mask=ni.DenseNeuroVol(mask.data, wrong))
```

A wrong-frame mask raises `ValueError` (`spatial contract mismatch`)
before any numeric work — the failed call leaves the BOLD untouched.

## See also

- [Start 05 — Round-trip](../start/05-roundtrip.qmd)
- [Spec: Receipt NIfTI extension](../spec/receipt-nifti-extension.html)
- [Concept: Provenance](../concepts/provenance.qmd)
